name: iMax Credit Repair Los Angeles
description_short: iMax Credit Repair uses attorney-led litigation and disputes to remove negative items from credit reports. They charge $0 upfront and only collect fees after achieving results.
description_long:
iMax Credit Repair is a Los Angeles-based credit repair firm that has operated for approximately 19 years. The company positions itself as an aggressive alternative to traditional credit repair by employing litigation and arbitration strategies rather than relying solely on standard credit bureau disputes. According to their website, they have maintained zero complaints with the Better Business Bureau throughout their operational history and claim to have helped thousands of clients improve their credit scores.

iMax offers credit repair services designed to remove negative items and maximize credit scores within 45 to 180 days. Their core service model is built on a results-based fee structure: clients pay $0 upfront and only pay after the company achieves deletions or improvements. They explicitly state they do not use traditional credit bureau disputes, arguing this method is ineffective due to automated verification systems. Instead, they employ associate attorneys to file lawsuits and arbitrations against creditors and collection companies. The company also provides free credit analysis to prospective clients and offers customized solutions rather than standardized packages.

The company differentiates itself through several claimed advantages: zero upfront fees (contrasting with competitors they cite charging $100 upfront), attorney-led litigation, a results-only payment guarantee, 5-star Yelp reviews, and claims of zero BBB complaints over 19 years. They emphasize that unlike other credit repair companies, they do not bill clients if they fail to produce results. The company also highlights security credentials, stating they are registered with the California Department of Justice, licensed, bonded, and registered, with claims of never experiencing a security breach.

Key limitations exist: fees can vary significantly (from a few hundred to several thousand dollars) depending on case complexity, making precise cost expectations difficult. Success is not guaranteed—the company acknowledges some clients will not see results. The aggressive litigation approach may not be suitable for all consumers or debt types. Additionally, while they claim competitive advantages over Lexington Law and Credit Saint, independent verification of their comparative effectiveness is not provided on the website.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: $0 upfront fees with results-based payment structure only
Attorney-led litigation and arbitration against creditors, not just disputes
Claims 19 years of zero BBB complaints
Free credit analysis before committing to services
Customized solutions tailored to individual client needs
No billing if results are not achieved
cons: Fees vary widely (few hundred to several thousand dollars) with no fixed pricing transparency
Timeline is lengthy—45 to 180 days, with no guarantee of success within that window
Company acknowledges it may not succeed on some clients' cases, leaving them with no charge but also no improvement
Litigation strategy may not be appropriate or effective for all negative items or creditor types
Claims about BBB record, Yelp reviews, and security are unverified on the profile
best_for: Consumers with multiple negative items willing to wait 45-180 days for potential removal
Clients with sufficient budgets for attorney-led litigation (several hundred to thousands of dollars)
People who prefer aggressive legal action over standard dispute processes
Consumers confident enough in their case that paying after results aligns with their risk tolerance
diagnosis: iMax Credit Repair is best for consumers with documented negative items on their credit reports who have the budget and patience for attorney-led litigation over 45-180 days and are comfortable with variable fee structures. The main caveat is that success is not guaranteed, and while they charge nothing if unsuccessful, there is no alternative compensation or recourse if their legal strategy fails.
services: Debt consolidation and balance reduction guidance
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 6555 Barton Ave Floor 2, Los Angeles, CA 90038
